rulings.Case Title: YITONG WEN VS. INTERNAL REVENUE SERVICES
rulings.Calendar Matter: ***Re-Notice *** Notice Of Hearing To Remove The Invalide Nominee Lien / Cloud On Title
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for October 23, 2023 line 1. PETITIONER YITONG WEN ***Re-Notice *** Notice Of Hearing To Remove The Invalid Nominee Lien / Cloud On Title is DENIED without prejudice. There is no proof of service on file for the Petition filed on May 31, 2023. The Court notes that based on the allegations in the Petition the title owner of the property is a corporation, which cannot be self-represented. The Court further notes that the Notice of Hearing is not supported by any evidence or applicable authority supporting the request to "quiet title remove cloud on title." =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (551) 285-1373; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: CHL INTERNATIONAL ASSOCIATES, A CALIFORNIA VS. CHITO KUMAR SAHA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Cross-Defendant Vikrant Punj And His Counsel And Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for October 23, 2023 line 2. DEFENDANT CHITO SAHA Notice Of Motion To Compel Cross-Defendant Vikrant Punj And His Counsel And Request For Sanctions is continued to October 27, 2023. No later than October 24, 2023 by 5:00 p.m. moving party to (1) submit a copy of the redacted documents subject to this motion with bates stamps visible on every page; (2) submit a privilege log including each item subject to this motion identified by corresponding bates stamp number; and (3) submit a declaration addressing moving party's argument that any part of Ex. G was improperly unredacted. The format of such declaration must be as follows: bates stamp number of the document page; unredacted portion (screenshot acceptable); reason that text should remain redacted. No later than October 23, 2023 by 5:00 p.m. opposing party to comply with SFRL 2.7B and provide a courtesy copy of opposition with a cover letter reflecting the new hearing date. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (551) 285-1373; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: CEDRIC BERMOND VS. JOSE TORRES ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT And In The Alternative Motion For Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for October 23, 2023 line 5. PLAINTIFF CEDRIC BERMOND MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT And In The Alternative Motion For Summary Adjudication is GRANTED, subject to opposition. The Court received opposition. However, Declaration of Jose Torres forming the basis of opposition (in part) cannot be considered by the court as it is submitted in violation of the Rules of Court and Local Rules: 2.5-inch thick opposition is not bound. Furthermore, Memorandum of Points and Authorities (in part appear to not be addressing the case before the Court, e.g. on page 11 of the opposition Defendant references "the defendant of discrimination (No.28)." According to the operative Answer on file twenty-eighth affirmative defense is not a defense of discrimination. Since it is impossible to determine which parts of the opposition are relevant to this case and which are not, no part of the opposition will be considered. Defendant may present oral opposition. =(501/SKF) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (551) 285-1373; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.
